purpose: HTTP server entry point that wires together SSEManager, DashboardService, WatcherService, QueueService, and apiRoutes into a single pure-Node `http` server (no framework). On every request it sets permissive CORS headers, logs non-SSE/non-OPTIONS requests on response finish, handles preflight OPTIONS with 204, and dispatches to either the `/events` SSE endpoint or the apiRoutes handler. The `/events` handler is the live dashboard pipe: it sets `text/event-stream` headers, optionally honors a `?dashboard=` query param to filter events, then immediately replays state to the new client via `dashboards_list`, `initialization`, `all_progress`, `init_state`, and `queue_changed` events before registering the response with `addClient`. The `startup()` function (passed as the listen callback) creates dashboards/, Archive/, history/, queue/ dirs if missing, ensures at least `dashboard1` exists, attaches per-dashboard watchers, starts the dashboards-dir watcher, queue-dir watcher, periodic reconciliation loop, and SSE heartbeat — all wired with `broadcast` from SSEManager as the change sink. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM stops watchers, heartbeat, closes all SSE clients, then `server.close()` and `process.exit(0)`.
gotchas: SSE responses must skip request logging on `res.on('finish')` — they are long-lived and would log incorrectly; the early `return` on `req.url.startsWith('/events')` is what guards this.
OPTIONS preflight must short-circuit with 204 BEFORE URL parsing — otherwise CORS preflights from browser clients fail.
`new URL(req.url, 'http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}')` requires a base because `req.url` is path-only; bare `new URL(req.url)` would throw.
Initial state replay sends `initialization` + `all_progress` separately AND a combined `init_state` — frontend code must dedupe or it will receive overlapping payloads on connect.
`startup()` is passed directly as the `server.listen` callback — it must be sync-safe; any async work must self-manage via promises or it can race with first incoming requests.
`process.exit(0)` in shutdown is called immediately after `server.close()` without awaiting its callback — in-flight SSE/HTTP responses may be aborted abruptly.
patterns: Pure-http server (no Express/Koa) — every endpoint is a manual `if (url.pathname === ... && req.method === ...)` branch.
API routing is delegated: `handleApiRoute(req, res, url)` returns boolean indicating whether the route was handled, and the SSE branch + 404 fallback are local.
Per-client dashboard filtering via `?dashboard=` query param stored in client metadata, used later by `broadcast` to skip non-matching clients.

On-connect state replay: send dashboards_list -> per-dashboard initialization -> per-dashboard progress -> per-dashboard combined init_state -> queue_changed, so a fresh client renders without polling.
Startup directory bootstrapping (`dashboards/`, `Archive/`, `history/`, `queue/`) is idempotent — all use `fs.existsSync` + `mkdirSync({recursive:true})`.
Graceful shutdown stops watchers BEFORE heartbeat BEFORE SSE close — mirrors the reverse order of startup.
conventions: Console logs use a leading two-space indent (e.g. `  [http] ...`, `  Synapse server listening...`) for visual grouping in terminal output.
Default dashboard ID is the literal string 'dashboard1' when none exist.
PORT defaults to 3456 (from constants) but is overridable via `PORT` env var.
